formula_latex: \log_{10}(E) = k_1 + k_2 \log_{10}(I_{arc}) + k_3 \log_{10}(G) + k_4 I_{arc} + k_5 I_{arc}^2 + k_6 G \cdot I_{arc} + k_7 V + k_8 V \cdot I_{arc} + k_9 V \cdot G + k_{10} \log_{10}(t) + k_{11} \log_{10}(D) + k_{12} V \cdot \log_{10}(t) + k_{13} V \cdot \log_{10}(D)
formula_plain_text: log10(E) = k1 + k2·log10(I_arc) + k3·log10(G) + k4·I_arc + k5·I_arc^2 + k6·G·I_arc + k7·V + k8·V·I_arc + k9·V·G + k10·log10(t) + k11·log10(D) + k12·V·log10(t) + k13·V·log10(D). The 2018 incident-energy model is a 13-term polynomial-in-V with cross-terms; coefficient ROW (k1..k13) is selected per (voltage_class, electrode_config) per Table 3. Note this functional form has explicit V coupling — distinguishes from the 2002 Annex F simplified regression which is purely log-linear with no V cross-terms. The 2018 SUPERSEDES 2002 Annex F for new studies.

symbols.meaning: Incident energy at working distance D
Arcing time (OCPD clearing time at I_arc)
Empirical coefficients per voltage class × electrode config (Table 3)

implementation_notes: IEEE 1584-2018 expresses V in kV and I_arc/I_bf in kA inside the polynomial (per Tables 1 and 3 unit conventions). Implementers MUST verify the unit convention against the licensed standard before computing — using V in volts will produce wildly wrong results given the V coupling in k7..k9, k12, k13. The two-stage flow is: (1) compute I_arc per arc-current-formula-2018.json + Table 1; (2) apply this formula with Table 3 coefficients to get E. Adjustment factors (gap-out-of-table, distance-out-of-table, enclosure-size) multiply the result per §10.5.
transcribed_by: DraftsMan skills repo — Sprint A.3 (Path Z2-ext)
license_note: Formula structure (13-term polynomial-in-V with cross-terms) is factual data per IEEE Std 1584-2018 Table 3 row schema. Coefficient values live in method-2018-tables-1-3-<class>V-coefficients.json (currently null pending source-vetted transcription).
